column1: # Existing L1 pool already has 11 cards (combat/utility/defense/control/reaction).
# This file adds the missing tiers.
# ===== L1 Core (1 new; fills gap to 12) =====

column3: Passive: once per encounter, the first time an ally's weapon or augment would break, the Forge Tender hears it cry and the break is held off one round.
Draw a slow breath and exhale onto an ally's gear; their next SIGNAL or RESONANCE check this round burns cleaner and hotter.
Whisper the true name of a foe's weapon or armor; while you hold the name, your strikes against that piece bite deeper and it resists its wielder.

column1: # ===== L3 Subclass: Resonance Keeper (4 cards) — living forges, intent over flame =====

column3: Place a hand on a blade and pour RESONANCE into it; the metal glows without flame and the next ally attack with it lands as searing.
Tap a foe's armor at its resonant note; the plate rings like a struck bell and nearby allies hear where it is weakest.
Plant your forge-stake on the ground; while it stands, allies within reach repair passively at the start of each round.
Run your palm along an ally's weapon; it sheds fatigue and sings. Their next attack this turn cannot be parried.

column1: # ===== L3 Subclass: Black Smith (4 cards) — cursed metalwork =====

column3: Feed a drop of your own blood to an ally's blade; it hungers for one round and each hit it lands draws a little back for you.
Lay a finger on a foe's weapon; it browns and flakes under your nail. Their next attack does less and may break on impact.
Forge a shard from something that should not be worked — bone, void-iron, a memory. Hand it to an ally as a one-use cursed weapon that hits harder but frays its wielder.
Drive a black nail into the ground at a fallen creature; if it tries to rise, stand, or reanimate before the round ends, it is pinned in place.

column1: # ===== L3 Subclass: Echomind Anchor (4 cards) — mind-imprint bindings =====

column3: Press an ally's palm to a fresh-forged hilt; the weapon learns their hand, and for the rest of the scene cannot be disarmed from them.
Speak the maker's name over an ally's dropped or thrown weapon; it returns to their hand as if remembering the way.
Bind two weapons to a single intent; when one ally strikes, a bound second weapon in another ally's hand echoes the swing at a nearby target.
Touch a foe's weapon and show it a truer maker; for one round it refuses to harm its wielder's allies and flinches from its own wielder.
Open the seventh ore-vein inside yourself; for one round your touch mends armor, sharpens edges, and closes wounds as a single gesture across all adjacent allies.
Plunge a burning weapon into cold resonance; a chosen ally's weapon ignores armor for their next attack, then smokes and goes quiet for the rest of the scene.
Lay both hands on a crafted object — weapon, door, chain, idol — and unmake it back to ore. Works on anything forged, no matter how sacred or cursed, once per rest.

column1: # ===== L9 Subclass Power: Resonance Keeper (2 cards) =====

column3: Strike the ground and wake every piece of forged metal in the field; for one round, every ally weapon rings in unison and lands with the voice of them all.
Set your own heartbeat as the rhythm of the forge; while it beats, one chosen ally weapon cannot cool, cannot dull, and cannot be dropped.

column1: # ===== L9 Subclass Power: Black Smith (2 cards) =====

column3: Pull a suit of armor out of the earth where no armor was buried; any ally who dons it gains frightful defense for the scene but leaves a piece of themselves behind when they take it off.
Drive the black nail through a foe's weapon into the ground; the weapon is pinned, the wielder cannot pick it up, and any oath or binding the weapon carried is broken.

column1: # ===== L9 Subclass Power: Echomind Anchor (2 cards) =====

column3: Call the imprinted mind of a dead maker into an ally's weapon; for one round the weapon fights alongside the wielder as a second set of hands.
Sever a foe's bond to their signature weapon or augment; for the rest of the scene the gear treats them as a stranger, failing, jamming, or refusing them outright.
